title: Elasticsearch and OpenSearch Ingest Pipeline Processor Failure Rate, Dead-Letter Queue Volume, and Document-Drop Forensics for Cross-Stack Logging Reliability
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.2.1 ITSI Service Health Score trending, UC-13.2.2 KPI degradation alerting, UC-13.2.3 episode MTTR analytics, UC-13.2.5 KPI base search performance economics, UC-13.2.6 Event Analytics rules engine fidelity, UC-13.2.7 predictive service health quality, UC-13.2.8 Glass Table NOC reliability, or UC-13.2.18 adaptive thresholding. Does not cover Lucene segment merge tuning without ingest processor context, Beats disconnect storms without pipeline failures, or Splunk parsing queues without Elasticsearch ingest evidence.
evidence: Saved search uc_13_2_9_es_opensearch_ingest_pipeline_reliability on a fifteen-minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns on es_metrics, es_logs, es_dlq, and otel_collector lanes; weekly CSV exports of elasticsearch_pipeline_inventory.csv and cross_stack_log_volume_baseline.csv archived to a restricted governance index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab Elasticsearch cluster, introduce a controlled grok mismatch on a non-production ingest pipeline, confirm documents.failed rises in index=es_metrics, processor exceptions appear in index=es_logs sourcetype=elasticsearch:server, and uc_13_2_9_es_opensearch_ingest_pipeline_reliability returns warn or page with non-null recommended_action within fifteen minutes.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery when node stats show zero failures for tested pipelines, DLQ bytes hold flat, server logs lack processor errors, and OTel mirrors Splunk within tolerance, filtering the saved search to page and warn only yields zero rows for four consecutive schedules.

dataSources: (index=es_metrics sourcetype IN ("elastic:node_stats","elasticsearch:node_stats","es:node_ingest","elasticsearch:ingest")) for GET _nodes/stats/ingest snapshots with pipeline.id, documents.total, documents.failed, node.id, cluster.name; (index=es_logs sourcetype=elasticsearch:server) for processor failures, failure.reason, script.compile_errors, _ingest/pipeline HTTP 4xx/5xx hints; (index=es_dlq) for failed-events-* and *-dlq-* inventory with index.name, dlq.bytes, dlq.docs, tenant_id; (index=otel_collector sourcetype IN ("otelcol","otel:metrics","splunk_otel_collector")) for exporter counters; lookup elasticsearch_pipeline_inventory.csv; lookup cross_stack_log_volume_baseline.csv; REST GET _ingest/pipeline/{id} for ground-truth processor graphs
app: Splunk Add-on for Elasticsearch (custom HTTP Event Collector normalization) + OpenTelemetry Collector
spl:
`comment("UC-13.2.9 Elasticsearch OpenSearch ingest pipeline failure rate DLQ growth OTel cross-stack reconciliation earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=es_metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now (sourcetype=elastic:node_stats OR sourcetype=elasticsearch:node_stats OR sourcetype=es:node_ingest OR sourcetype=elasticsearch:ingest)
      | eval cluster_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('cluster.name',cluster_name,cluster,""))))
      | eval node_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('node.id',node_id,host,""))))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('pipeline.id',pipeline_id,pipeline,""))))
      | eval proc_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('processor.type',processor_type,"ingest_aggregate"))))
      | eval proc_tag=trim(toString(coalesce('processor.tag',processor_tag,"")))
      | eval doc_total=tonumber(coalesce('documents.total',documents_total,0))
      | eval doc_failed=tonumber(coalesce('documents.failed',documents_failed,0))
      | eval fail_reason="node_stats_ingest_counters"
      | eval script_compile_errors=tonumber(coalesce('script.compile_errors',script_compile_errors,0))
      | eval idx_name="metrics_lane"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,"__cluster__"))))
      | eval dlq_bytes=0
      | eval dlq_docs=0
      | eval otel_exporter_events=0
      | eval lane="es_metrics_ingest"
      | eval http_ingest_status=0
      | fields _time cluster_name node_id pipeline_id proc_type proc_tag doc_total doc_failed fail_reason script_compile_errors idx_name tenant_id dlq_bytes dlq_docs otel_exporter_events lane http_ingest_status ]
    [ search index=es_logs sourcetype=elasticsearch:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"ingest|pipeline|processor|grok|dissect|script|geoip|dot_expander|on_failure|_ingest/pipeline")
      | eval cluster_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('cluster.name',cluster_name,cluster,""))))
      | eval node_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('node.id',node_id,host,""))))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('pipeline.id',pipeline_id,""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)pipeline[^a-z0-9_]*(?<rex_pipeline>[a-z0-9_\\.\\-]{2,128})"
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(coalesce(pipeline_id,rex_pipeline,"__unknown_pipeline__")))
      | eval proc_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('processor.type',processor_type,"processor_error"))))
      | eval proc_tag=trim(toString(coalesce('processor.tag',processor_tag,"")))
      | eval doc_total=tonumber(coalesce('documents.total',documents_total,0))
      | eval doc_failed=tonumber(coalesce('documents.failed',documents_failed,1))
      | eval fail_reason=substr(trim(toString(coalesce('failure.reason',failure_reason,message,"processor_exception"))),1,256)
      | eval script_compile_errors=tonumber(coalesce('script.compile_errors',script_compile_errors,if(match(lr,"compile error|compilation failed"),1,0)))
      | eval idx_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('index.name',index_name,target_index,""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,service_tenant,"__cluster__"))))
      | eval dlq_bytes=0
      | eval dlq_docs=0
      | eval otel_exporter_events=0
      | eval lane="es_server_processor_logs"
      | eval http_ingest_status=if(match(lr,"status=4\\d\\d|\\\"status\\\":4|http[_ ]?response[_ ]?code=4"),400,if(match(lr,"status=5\\d\\d|\\\"status\\\":5|http[_ ]?response[_ ]?code=5"),500,0))
      | fields _time cluster_name node_id pipeline_id proc_type proc_tag doc_total doc_failed fail_reason script_compile_errors idx_name tenant_id dlq_bytes dlq_docs otel_exporter_events lane http_ingest_status ]
    [ search index=es_dlq earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('cluster.name',cluster_name,cluster,""))))
      | eval node_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('node.id',node_id,"dlq_index"))))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('pipeline.id',pipeline_id,""))))
      | rex field=index "(?i)(?<dlq_pipe>[a-z0-9_\\.\\-]+)[\\-_]dlq|failed[_\\-]events[_\\-](?<dlq_pipe2>[a-z0-9_\\.\\-]+)"
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(coalesce(pipeline_id,dlq_pipe,dlq_pipe2,"dlq_inventory")))
      | eval proc_type="dlq_index"
      | eval proc_tag=trim(toString(coalesce('processor.tag',processor_tag,"dlq")))
      | eval doc_total=tonumber(coalesce('documents.total',documents_total,indexing_total,0))
      | eval doc_failed=tonumber(coalesce('documents.failed',documents_failed,0))
      | eval fail_reason="dead_letter_index_growth"
      | eval script_compile_errors=0
      | eval idx_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('index.name',index_name,index,""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,"__cluster__"))))
      | eval dlq_bytes=tonumber(coalesce('dlq.bytes',dlq_bytes,store_size_bytes,index_size_bytes,0))
      | eval dlq_docs=tonumber(coalesce('dlq.docs',dlq_docs,doc_count,0))
      | eval otel_exporter_events=0
      | eval lane="es_dlq_inventory"
      | eval http_ingest_status=0
      | fields _time cluster_name node_id pipeline_id proc_type proc_tag doc_total doc_failed fail_reason script_compile_errors idx_name tenant_id dlq_bytes dlq_docs otel_exporter_events lane http_ingest_status ]
    [ search index=otel_collector earliest=-4h@h latest=now (sourcetype=otelcol OR sourcetype=otel:metrics OR sourcetype=splunk_otel_collector)
      | eval cluster_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('cluster.name',cluster_name,k8s_cluster_name,"otel_mesh"))))
      | eval node_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('node.id',node_id,instance,"otel_collector"))))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('pipeline.id',pipeline_id,otel_pipeline,"cross_stack_export"))))
      | eval proc_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('processor.type',processor_type,"otel_batch_export"))))
      | eval proc_tag=trim(toString(coalesce('processor.tag',processor_tag,"otel")))
      | eval doc_total=tonumber(coalesce('documents.total',documents_total,otel_records_sent,0))
      | eval doc_failed=tonumber(coalesce('documents.failed',documents_failed,otel_records_failed,export_failures,0))
      | eval fail_reason=substr(trim(toString(coalesce('failure.reason',failure_reason,"otel_export"))),1,256)
      | eval script_compile_errors=0
      | eval idx_name="otel_exporter"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,service.namespace,"__cluster__"))))
      | eval dlq_bytes=0
      | eval dlq_docs=0
      | eval otel_exporter_events=tonumber(coalesce(exported_log_records,splunk_hec_events,otel_log_records_sent,doc_total,0))
      | eval lane="otel_collector_counters"
      | eval http_ingest_status=0
      | fields _time cluster_name node_id pipeline_id proc_type proc_tag doc_total doc_failed fail_reason script_compile_errors idx_name tenant_id dlq_bytes dlq_docs otel_exporter_events lane http_ingest_status ]
| eval doc_total=coalesce(doc_total,0)
| eval doc_failed=coalesce(doc_failed,0)
| eval fail_rate_pct=if(doc_total>0,round(100*doc_failed/doc_total,4),if(doc_failed>0,100,0))
| eval dlq_bytes=coalesce(dlq_bytes,0)
| eval dlq_docs=coalesce(dlq_docs,0)
| eval otel_exporter_events=coalesce(otel_exporter_events,0)
| eval script_compile_errors=coalesce(script_compile_errors,0)
| eval http_ingest_status=coalesce(http_ingest_status,0)
| sort 0 cluster_name pipeline_id proc_type - _time
| streamstats window=6 current=t global=f avg(fail_rate_pct) AS fail_rate_ma BY cluster_name pipeline_id
| eventstats median(fail_rate_pct) AS cluster_median_fail_pct BY cluster_name
| join type=left max=0 pipeline_id
    [| inputlookup elasticsearch_pipeline_inventory.csv
     | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_id,pipeline,""))))
     | fields pipeline_id pipeline_owner on_failure_policy slo_tier expected_tenant_keys ]
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id
    [| inputlookup cross_stack_log_volume_baseline.csv
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,""))))
     | fields tenant_id baseline_eps expected_splunk_index expected_log_family ]
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id
    [| tstats count AS splunk_mirror_events WHERE index=* earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY tenant_id
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(tenant_id))) ]
| eval baseline_eps=coalesce(baseline_eps,0)
| eval splunk_mirror_events=coalesce(splunk_mirror_events,0)
| eval baseline_expected=baseline_eps*14400
| eval cross_stack_gap_pct=if(baseline_expected>0 AND splunk_mirror_events>0,round(abs(baseline_expected-splunk_mirror_events)/if(baseline_expected>splunk_mirror_events,baseline_expected,splunk_mirror_events)*100,2),null())
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen latest(doc_total) AS doc_total latest(doc_failed) AS doc_failed max(fail_rate_pct) AS fail_rate_pct max(fail_rate_ma) AS fail_rate_ma max(cluster_median_fail_pct) AS cluster_median_fail_pct max(dlq_bytes) AS dlq_bytes max(dlq_docs) AS dlq_docs max(otel_exporter_events) AS otel_exporter_events max(splunk_mirror_events) AS splunk_mirror_events max(cross_stack_gap_pct) AS cross_stack_gap_pct values(proc_type) AS proc_type_mv values(fail_reason) AS fail_reason_mv max(script_compile_errors) AS script_compile_errors max(http_ingest_status) AS http_ingest_status first(on_failure_policy) AS on_failure_policy first(slo_tier) AS slo_tier first(baseline_eps) AS baseline_eps first(expected_log_family) AS expected_log_family BY cluster_name pipeline_id tenant_id lane
| eval proc_type=mvindex(mvdedup(proc_type_mv),0)
| eval fail_reason=mvindex(mvdedup(fail_reason_mv),0)
| eval baseline_expected=baseline_eps*14400
| eval fail_rate_pct=if(doc_total>0,round(100*doc_failed/doc_total,4),if(doc_failed>0,100,fail_rate_pct))
| eval cross_stack_gap_pct=if(baseline_expected>0 AND splunk_mirror_events>0,round(abs(baseline_expected-splunk_mirror_events)/if(baseline_expected>splunk_mirror_events,baseline_expected,splunk_mirror_events)*100,2),cross_stack_gap_pct)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(http_ingest_status,0)>=500,"page",
    coalesce(script_compile_errors,0)>0,"page",
    coalesce(fail_rate_pct,0)>=5 OR coalesce(dlq_bytes,0)>1073741824,"page",
    match(lower(proc_type),"grok|dissect|script|geoip|dot_expander") AND coalesce(fail_rate_pct,0)>=1,"warn",
    coalesce(fail_rate_pct,0)>=1 OR coalesce(dlq_docs,0)>=100000,"warn",
    isnotnull(cross_stack_gap_pct) AND cross_stack_gap_pct>=25,"warn",
    coalesce(http_ingest_status,0)>=400,"warn",
    true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="page" AND coalesce(script_compile_errors,0)>0,"remediate_script_processor_source_fix_painless_compile_errors_then_rolling_restart_ingest_path",
    severity=="page" AND coalesce(http_ingest_status,0)>=500,"inspect_coordinating_node_logs_and_load_for_ingest_pipeline_api_5xx_then_validate_disk_and_threadpools",
    severity=="page" AND coalesce(dlq_bytes,0)>1073741824,"expand_dlq_retention_policy_replay_failed_events_after_parser_fix_validate_disk_watermarks",
    severity=="warn" AND match(lower(proc_type),"grok|dissect"),"tighten_grok_or_dissect_patterns_add_on_failure_routing_to_named_dlq_index_with_processor_tag_forensics",
    severity=="warn" AND match(lower(proc_type),"geoip|dot_expander"),"validate_geoip_database_mounts_and_dot_expander_path_conflicts_on_nested_fields",
    isnotnull(cross_stack_gap_pct) AND cross_stack_gap_pct>=25,"reconcile_otel_exporter_counters_against_splunk_mirror_counts_verify_duplicate_hosts_and_sampling_divergence",
    true(),"continue_hourly_pipeline_inventory_reconciliation_and_processor_dashboard_review")
| fields - proc_type_mv fail_reason_mv baseline_eps
| table cluster_name pipeline_id tenant_id lane proc_type fail_rate_pct fail_rate_ma cluster_median_fail_pct doc_total doc_failed dlq_bytes dlq_docs otel_exporter_events splunk_mirror_events cross_stack_gap_pct fail_reason script_compile_errors http_ingest_status on_failure_policy slo_tier expected_log_family severity recommended_action last_seen
description: Detects Elasticsearch and OpenSearch index-time ingest pipeline reliability failures by fusing per-pipeline documents.failed versus documents.total failure rates from node ingest stats, dead-letter index growth from failed-events-* and pipeline-scoped DLQ patterns, parser-class signals for grok dissect script geoip and dot_expander in server logs including processor.tag forensics, painless compile error hints, _ingest/pipeline REST error narratives, OpenTelemetry exporter throughput mismatched against Splunk-side tenant volumes, and governance joins for intentional on_failure drop policies.
value: Platform observability leaders shorten mean time to innocence when the logging mesh silently loses documents at index time while dashboards still show green cluster health, security operations teams retain defensible proof of which tenant and which processor step diverged, and executives see cross-stack reconciliation that explains whether loss lives in the Elastic ingest path versus the OpenTelemetry fork feeding Splunk.
implementation: Land _nodes/stats/ingest snapshots in index=es_metrics, server processor logs in index=es_logs sourcetype=elasticsearch:server, DLQ inventory in index=es_dlq, and OTel exporter counters in index=otel_collector; maintain elasticsearch_pipeline_inventory.csv and cross_stack_log_volume_baseline.csv; save uc_13_2_9_es_opensearch_ingest_pipeline_reliability every fifteen minutes with earliest=-4h@h; route page and warn via the observability queue with drill links to pipeline definitions, DLQ indices, and OTel pipelines.
visualization: Severity-colored table by cluster_name, pipeline_id, and tenant_id with drilldowns to es_metrics ingest stats, elasticsearch:server exceptions, es_dlq index rows, otel_collector exporter series, cross_stack_gap_pct tiles, and lookup governance columns; timeline of fail_rate_pct with fail_rate_ma overlay; single-value DLQ bytes.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=f count FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS node_hint
| table node_hint count
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance summaries for hosts that run Elasticsearch coordinating nodes, OpenSearch cluster managers, and OpenTelemetry Collectors when you map those hosts into Performance.host for infra correlation; retain thirty days for quarterly ingest attestations.

references.title: Elastic — Handling failures in ingest pipelines
OpenSearch Documentation — Create or update ingest pipeline
Splunk Lantern — Observability best practices hub
Splunk Docs — Get data into Splunk (HEC and agent patterns)
Elastic — Bulk API and partial failures

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate parsing edge-case bursts arrive with every major application rollout when new log lines do not yet match grok or dissect patterns; hold a short dwell window and correlate with change tickets before paging product teams. Some pipelines intentionally route grok failures into a DLQ index for forensic sampling; read on_failure_policy in elasticsearch_pipeline_inventory.csv before treating DLQ growth as an incident. GeoIP lookup misses are common for internal RFC1918 addresses and expected when processors guard public-ip-only enrichment; pair failures with network address plans. Planned schema migrations and reindex jobs can temporarily inflate documents.failed counters or DLQ documents during controlled replays; compare cluster maintenance calendars and ILM phases. Amazon OpenSearch Service blue-green maintenance can shift ingest threadpools without application changes; validate service events before blaming parsers. OpenTelemetry exporter counters can exceed mirror counts when Splunk applies stronger filtering or sampling at the HEC tier while Elasticsearch ingests raw volume; tune cross_stack_gap_pct thresholds after documenting intentional differences. Coordinating node log regexes for HTTP status can false-positive on unrelated access logs that mention pipeline keywords; scope sourcetype and logger filters tightly. Duplicate tenant_id labels across dev and prod test hosts can skew tstats joins until lookups segregate environments. Metricbeat upgrades sometimes rename dotted fields; coalesce ladders may mask partial collection until FIELDALIAS rules catch up.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — time-align ingest loss windows with service episodes when Elasticsearch backs an ITSI entity KPI

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the observability engineers who operate Elasticsearch and OpenSearch clusters that sit inside the logging mesh, the SRE teams that curate ingest pipelines and dead-letter indices, and the Splunk platform owners who receive the same log families through OpenTelemetry Collector exports. This use case isolates index-time ingest pipelines registered under the _ingest API, not generic cluster disk alarms, not Beats shipper disconnects in isolation, and not Splunk HTTP Event Collector endpoint saturation by itself. Ingest pipelines execute a ordered list of processors such as grok, dissect, script, geoip, dot_expander, set, remove, rename, pipeline, and failure handlers that can silently drop documents when on_failure routes to a drop processor or when processors swallow exceptions without a durable dead-letter path.

Scope boundaries are explicit so reviewers do not duplicate sibling gold use cases under cat-13.2. UC-13.2.1 remains Splunk ITSI Service Health Score trending with NEAP correlation and multi-window regression on itsi_summary without interpreting Elasticsearch processor counters. UC-13.2.2 remains KPI degradation alerting on ITSI threshold semantics rather than Elastic ingest processor failure rates. UC-13.2.3 remains episode volume and mean time to resolve analytics on grouped alerts. UC-13.2.5 remains KPI base search performance economics on the Splunk search tier. UC-13.2.6 remains Event Analytics rules engine fidelity. UC-13.2.7 remains predictive service health forecast quality. UC-13.2.8 remains ITSI Glass Table NOC display reliability. UC-13.2.18 remains adaptive threshold calibration inside ITSI. None of those siblings answer whether a grok processor on pipeline ent-log-security-001 is failing one percent of documents, whether a failed-events-* index doubled in byte size overnight, whether painless compile errors appeared after a cluster upgrade, or whether OpenTelemetry exporter counters diverge from Splunk-side counts for the same tenant.

Elasticsearch exposes per-node ingest statistics through GET _nodes/stats/ingest including pipeline-level documents counts and failed document counts that you should normalize into fields such as documents.total and documents.failed for each pipeline.id and node.id. OpenSearch follows the same ingest pipeline processor model for compatibility and adds enterprise features on managed services such as Amazon OpenSearch Service where you still need API or Cat indices visibility for dead-letter patterns. Processor failures surface in server logs as structured or semi-structured lines that include processor.type, processor.tag when authors set tags for observability, and failure.reason strings that distinguish grok match failures from script exceptions. Silent drops happen when on_failure handlers chain to a drop processor or when clients use bulk settings that skip detailed per-item error reporting while still accepting HTTP 200 responses.

Cross-stack reconciliation matters because many estates dual-feed logs: Elasticsearch or OpenSearch for search-heavy analytics, and Splunk for security operations or governed retention through OpenTelemetry Collector pipelines that fork the same OTLP or file tail sources. When Splunk mirror counts fall materially below exporter counters after controlling for sampling, the gap often traces to Elasticsearch-side ingest loss, mis-typed bulk acknowledgements, or tenant routing mistakes rather than forwarder outages alone.

Role based access for collectors hitting _nodes/stats/ingest, _cat/indices for failed-events-* and *-dlq-* patterns, and _ingest/pipeline should use least-privilege Elasticsearch roles such as monitor and manage_index_templates only where required, not superuser keys on laptops. Store credentials in vault-backed modular inputs or short-lived workload identities on Kubernetes. Legal reviewers may care that dead-letter documents can contain payloads with personal data; restrict index=es_dlq retention and mask high-cardinality fields at ingest when policy demands.

Index design: land node stats snapshots in index=es_metrics with sourcetypes such as elastic:node_stats or elasticsearch:node_stats at five-minute intervals for steady-state and one-minute during change windows. Land coordinating and data node logs in index=es_logs sourcetype=elasticsearch:server with LINE_BREAKER rules that preserve multiline stack traces for script processor failures. Land dead-letter inventory metrics in index=es_dlq derived from _cat/indices or index stats API exports that include store size and document counts for indices matching failed-events-* and *-dlq-* conventions. Land OpenTelemetry Collector internal telemetry in index=otel_collector with sourcetypes such as otelcol or splunk_otel_collector that expose exporter success and failure counters. Retention should cover at least fourteen days hot for ingest forensics and thirty days for change correlation after major upgrades.

Field normalization: flatten cluster.name and node.id onto every event, map pipeline.id consistently, preserve processor.type and processor.tag, map failure.reason text, map script.compile_errors style counters when your exporter emits them, map index.name for DLQ targets, map tenant_id from metadata you already stamp for multi-tenant logging, and map dlq.bytes plus dlq.docs when you summarize index size and document totals for DLQ indices. Lookup elasticsearch_pipeline_inventory.csv should list pipeline_id, pipeline_owner, on_failure_policy, slo_tier, and expected_tenant_keys. Lookup cross_stack_log_volume_baseline.csv should list tenant_id, baseline_eps, expected_splunk_index, and expected_log_family for cross-stack math.

Risk briefing: grok and dissect failures spike during application rollouts that introduce new log lines; require dwell and baseline windows before paging application teams. GeoIP processors legitimately miss private RFC1918 addresses; pair misses with expectations for internal-only sources. Planned schema migrations can intentionally raise failure rates in canary tenants; read change calendars before executive escalation. Amazon OpenSearch Service maintenance can shift threadpool behavior; correlate with service events before blaming application parsers.

Differentiation recap: Elasticsearch and OpenSearch ingest pipeline processor failure rates, DLQ index growth forensics, parser-class attribution, on_failure and silent drop awareness, script compile error detection, _ingest API error hints in logs, OpenTelemetry cross-stack reconciliation, and tenant attribution—not ITSI health scores, not KPI base search scheduler economics alone, not Glass Table rendering, not generic JVM heap alarms without ingest context.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four complementary lanes that Splunk multisearch can fuse for this control, plus lookup maintenance for governance joins.

Lane es_metrics_ingest polls GET _nodes/stats/ingest on each data-eligible node or relies on Elastic Agent or Metricbeat style modules that already forward node stats. Normalize pipeline sections so each event carries cluster.name, node.id, pipeline.id, documents.total, documents.failed, and optional processor-level counters when you expand stats beyond totals. Emit sourcetype=elastic:node_stats or elasticsearch:node_stats into index=es_metrics every five minutes in steady state. Include coordinating-only nodes when your architecture runs ingest pipelines on dedicated coordinating tiers.

Lane es_server_processor_logs ships elasticsearch server logs from /var/log/elasticsearch or managed service equivalents into index=es_logs sourcetype=elasticsearch:server. Preserve logger names, stack traces for script processor compile failures, and HTTP status lines for REST calls against _ingest/pipeline. Use props.conf to extract quoted JSON fragments when clusters log structured pipeline errors. Increase TRUNCATE for deep stack traces but redact secrets with SEDCMD when indices accidentally appear in error text.

Lane es_dlq_inventory schedules _cat/indices or Index Stats API calls filtered to failed-events-*, *-dlq-*, and internal patterns your catalog documents. Emit one event per index with index.name, dlq.bytes for store size in bytes, dlq.docs for primaries document count when you query primaries=true, tenant_id when encoded in index naming, and cluster.name. Land in index=es_dlq with a stable sourcetype such as opensearch:dlq_inventory or elasticsearch:dlq_inventory.

Lane otel_collector_counters scrapes OpenTelemetry Collector Prometheus exporters or file exporters that include otelcol_receiver_accepted_spans style metrics and Splunk exporter throughput when using splunk_hec exporter. Land in index=otel_collector with sourcetype=otel:metrics or splunk_otel_collector. Map tenant_id from resource attributes when possible so joins align to the same tenant_id keys used in cross_stack_log_volume_baseline.csv.

Lookup governance: version control elasticsearch_pipeline_inventory.csv and cross_stack_log_volume_baseline.csv, refresh at least daily from configuration management or REST inventory jobs. Include on_failure_policy text that states whether a pipeline drops, reindexes to DLQ, or tags only, so Splunk severity logic respects intentional designs.

Example curl helpers for design reviews:

```bash
curl -sS -H "Authorization: ApiKey $ES_API_KEY" "$ES_HOST/_nodes/stats/ingest?pretty"
curl -sS -H "Authorization: ApiKey $ES_API_KEY" "$ES_HOST/_cat/indices/failed-events-*?bytes=b&s=store.size:desc"
curl -sS -H "Authorization: ApiKey $ES_API_KEY" "$ES_HOST/_ingest/pipeline/my-pipeline?pretty"
```

Pre-save validation: index=es_metrics must return non-zero events within two collection intervals after enablement. index=es_logs sourcetype=elasticsearch:server must show processor exceptions during intentional lab mis-parses. index=es_dlq must move when you index synthetic documents into a test DLQ index. index=otel_collector must show exporter counters moving under load tests.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_2_9_es_opensearch_ingest_pipeline_reliability with a fifteen-minute schedule during production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is page or warn. Throttle duplicate cluster_name, pipeline_id, tenant_id triples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Include recommended_action, lane, and fail_reason in pager bodies so incident commanders open Elasticsearch and OpenSearch runbooks without guessing which processor class failed.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans es_metrics node ingest stats, elasticsearch server processor logs, es_dlq dead-letter inventory, and otel_collector exporter counters so one silent lane cannot hide genuine ingest loss. coalesce() ladders absorb flattened versus dotted field naming drift across exporters. streamstats computes short moving averages for per-pipeline fail_rate_pct baselines. eventstats adds cluster-level median context for fail_rate_pct. join enriches pipeline governance from elasticsearch_pipeline_inventory.csv and cross-stack baselines from cross_stack_log_volume_baseline.csv. A tstats arm estimates Splunk-side event totals per tenant_id for the same window so cross_stack_gap_pct highlights reconciliation risk. case() maps script compile errors, five hundreds from pipeline API narratives, high fail rates, DLQ byte explosions, parser-class failures, and OTel versus Splunk divergence into page versus warn versus info tiers.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.9 Elasticsearch OpenSearch ingest pipeline failure rate DLQ growth OTel cross-stack reconciliation earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=es_metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now (sourcetype=elastic:node_stats OR sourcetype=elasticsearch:node_stats OR sourcetype=es:node_ingest OR sourcetype=elasticsearch:ingest)
      | eval cluster_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('cluster.name',cluster_name,cluster,""))))
      | eval node_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('node.id',node_id,host,""))))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('pipeline.id',pipeline_id,pipeline,""))))
      | eval proc_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('processor.type',processor_type,"ingest_aggregate"))))
      | eval proc_tag=trim(toString(coalesce('processor.tag',processor_tag,"")))
      | eval doc_total=tonumber(coalesce('documents.total',documents_total,0))
      | eval doc_failed=tonumber(coalesce('documents.failed',documents_failed,0))
      | eval fail_reason="node_stats_ingest_counters"
      | eval script_compile_errors=tonumber(coalesce('script.compile_errors',script_compile_errors,0))
      | eval idx_name="metrics_lane"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,"__cluster__"))))
      | eval dlq_bytes=0
      | eval dlq_docs=0
      | eval otel_exporter_events=0
      | eval lane="es_metrics_ingest"
      | eval http_ingest_status=0
      | fields _time cluster_name node_id pipeline_id proc_type proc_tag doc_total doc_failed fail_reason script_compile_errors idx_name tenant_id dlq_bytes dlq_docs otel_exporter_events lane http_ingest_status ]
    [ search index=es_logs sourcetype=elasticsearch:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"ingest|pipeline|processor|grok|dissect|script|geoip|dot_expander|on_failure|_ingest/pipeline")
      | eval cluster_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('cluster.name',cluster_name,cluster,""))))
      | eval node_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('node.id',node_id,host,""))))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('pipeline.id',pipeline_id,""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)pipeline[^a-z0-9_]*(?<rex_pipeline>[a-z0-9_\\.\\-]{2,128})"
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(coalesce(pipeline_id,rex_pipeline,"__unknown_pipeline__")))
      | eval proc_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('processor.type',processor_type,"processor_error"))))
      | eval proc_tag=trim(toString(coalesce('processor.tag',processor_tag,"")))
      | eval doc_total=tonumber(coalesce('documents.total',documents_total,0))
      | eval doc_failed=tonumber(coalesce('documents.failed',documents_failed,1))
      | eval fail_reason=substr(trim(toString(coalesce('failure.reason',failure_reason,message,"processor_exception"))),1,256)
      | eval script_compile_errors=tonumber(coalesce('script.compile_errors',script_compile_errors,if(match(lr,"compile error|compilation failed"),1,0)))
      | eval idx_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('index.name',index_name,target_index,""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,service_tenant,"__cluster__"))))
      | eval dlq_bytes=0
      | eval dlq_docs=0
      | eval otel_exporter_events=0
      | eval lane="es_server_processor_logs"
      | eval http_ingest_status=if(match(lr,"status=4\\d\\d|\\\"status\\\":4|http[_ ]?response[_ ]?code=4"),400,if(match(lr,"status=5\\d\\d|\\\"status\\\":5|http[_ ]?response[_ ]?code=5"),500,0))
      | fields _time cluster_name node_id pipeline_id proc_type proc_tag doc_total doc_failed fail_reason script_compile_errors idx_name tenant_id dlq_bytes dlq_docs otel_exporter_events lane http_ingest_status ]
    [ search index=es_dlq earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('cluster.name',cluster_name,cluster,""))))
      | eval node_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('node.id',node_id,"dlq_index"))))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('pipeline.id',pipeline_id,""))))
      | rex field=index "(?i)(?<dlq_pipe>[a-z0-9_\\.\\-]+)[\\-_]dlq|failed[_\\-]events[_\\-](?<dlq_pipe2>[a-z0-9_\\.\\-]+)"
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(coalesce(pipeline_id,dlq_pipe,dlq_pipe2,"dlq_inventory")))
      | eval proc_type="dlq_index"
      | eval proc_tag=trim(toString(coalesce('processor.tag',processor_tag,"dlq")))
      | eval doc_total=tonumber(coalesce('documents.total',documents_total,indexing_total,0))
      | eval doc_failed=tonumber(coalesce('documents.failed',documents_failed,0))
      | eval fail_reason="dead_letter_index_growth"
      | eval script_compile_errors=0
      | eval idx_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('index.name',index_name,index,""))))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,"__cluster__"))))
      | eval dlq_bytes=tonumber(coalesce('dlq.bytes',dlq_bytes,store_size_bytes,index_size_bytes,0))
      | eval dlq_docs=tonumber(coalesce('dlq.docs',dlq_docs,doc_count,0))
      | eval otel_exporter_events=0
      | eval lane="es_dlq_inventory"
      | eval http_ingest_status=0
      | fields _time cluster_name node_id pipeline_id proc_type proc_tag doc_total doc_failed fail_reason script_compile_errors idx_name tenant_id dlq_bytes dlq_docs otel_exporter_events lane http_ingest_status ]
    [ search index=otel_collector earliest=-4h@h latest=now (sourcetype=otelcol OR sourcetype=otel:metrics OR sourcetype=splunk_otel_collector)
      | eval cluster_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('cluster.name',cluster_name,k8s_cluster_name,"otel_mesh"))))
      | eval node_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('node.id',node_id,instance,"otel_collector"))))
      | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('pipeline.id',pipeline_id,otel_pipeline,"cross_stack_export"))))
      | eval proc_type=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('processor.type',processor_type,"otel_batch_export"))))
      | eval proc_tag=trim(toString(coalesce('processor.tag',processor_tag,"otel")))
      | eval doc_total=tonumber(coalesce('documents.total',documents_total,otel_records_sent,0))
      | eval doc_failed=tonumber(coalesce('documents.failed',documents_failed,otel_records_failed,export_failures,0))
      | eval fail_reason=substr(trim(toString(coalesce('failure.reason',failure_reason,"otel_export"))),1,256)
      | eval script_compile_errors=0
      | eval idx_name="otel_exporter"
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,service.namespace,"__cluster__"))))
      | eval dlq_bytes=0
      | eval dlq_docs=0
      | eval otel_exporter_events=tonumber(coalesce(exported_log_records,splunk_hec_events,otel_log_records_sent,doc_total,0))
      | eval lane="otel_collector_counters"
      | eval http_ingest_status=0
      | fields _time cluster_name node_id pipeline_id proc_type proc_tag doc_total doc_failed fail_reason script_compile_errors idx_name tenant_id dlq_bytes dlq_docs otel_exporter_events lane http_ingest_status ]
| eval doc_total=coalesce(doc_total,0)
| eval doc_failed=coalesce(doc_failed,0)
| eval fail_rate_pct=if(doc_total>0,round(100*doc_failed/doc_total,4),if(doc_failed>0,100,0))
| eval dlq_bytes=coalesce(dlq_bytes,0)
| eval dlq_docs=coalesce(dlq_docs,0)
| eval otel_exporter_events=coalesce(otel_exporter_events,0)
| eval script_compile_errors=coalesce(script_compile_errors,0)
| eval http_ingest_status=coalesce(http_ingest_status,0)
| sort 0 cluster_name pipeline_id proc_type - _time
| streamstats window=6 current=t global=f avg(fail_rate_pct) AS fail_rate_ma BY cluster_name pipeline_id
| eventstats median(fail_rate_pct) AS cluster_median_fail_pct BY cluster_name
| join type=left max=0 pipeline_id
    [| inputlookup elasticsearch_pipeline_inventory.csv
     | eval pipeline_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_id,pipeline,""))))
     | fields pipeline_id pipeline_owner on_failure_policy slo_tier expected_tenant_keys ]
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id
    [| inputlookup cross_stack_log_volume_baseline.csv
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,tenant,""))))
     | fields tenant_id baseline_eps expected_splunk_index expected_log_family ]
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id
    [| tstats count AS splunk_mirror_events WHERE index=* earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY tenant_id
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(tenant_id))) ]
| eval baseline_eps=coalesce(baseline_eps,0)
| eval splunk_mirror_events=coalesce(splunk_mirror_events,0)
| eval baseline_expected=baseline_eps*14400
| eval cross_stack_gap_pct=if(baseline_expected>0 AND splunk_mirror_events>0,round(abs(baseline_expected-splunk_mirror_events)/if(baseline_expected>splunk_mirror_events,baseline_expected,splunk_mirror_events)*100,2),null())
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen latest(doc_total) AS doc_total latest(doc_failed) AS doc_failed max(fail_rate_pct) AS fail_rate_pct max(fail_rate_ma) AS fail_rate_ma max(cluster_median_fail_pct) AS cluster_median_fail_pct max(dlq_bytes) AS dlq_bytes max(dlq_docs) AS dlq_docs max(otel_exporter_events) AS otel_exporter_events max(splunk_mirror_events) AS splunk_mirror_events max(cross_stack_gap_pct) AS cross_stack_gap_pct values(proc_type) AS proc_type_mv values(fail_reason) AS fail_reason_mv max(script_compile_errors) AS script_compile_errors max(http_ingest_status) AS http_ingest_status first(on_failure_policy) AS on_failure_policy first(slo_tier) AS slo_tier first(baseline_eps) AS baseline_eps first(expected_log_family) AS expected_log_family BY cluster_name pipeline_id tenant_id lane
| eval proc_type=mvindex(mvdedup(proc_type_mv),0)
| eval fail_reason=mvindex(mvdedup(fail_reason_mv),0)
| eval baseline_expected=baseline_eps*14400
| eval fail_rate_pct=if(doc_total>0,round(100*doc_failed/doc_total,4),if(doc_failed>0,100,fail_rate_pct))
| eval cross_stack_gap_pct=if(baseline_expected>0 AND splunk_mirror_events>0,round(abs(baseline_expected-splunk_mirror_events)/if(baseline_expected>splunk_mirror_events,baseline_expected,splunk_mirror_events)*100,2),cross_stack_gap_pct)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(http_ingest_status,0)>=500,"page",
    coalesce(script_compile_errors,0)>0,"page",
    coalesce(fail_rate_pct,0)>=5 OR coalesce(dlq_bytes,0)>1073741824,"page",
    match(lower(proc_type),"grok|dissect|script|geoip|dot_expander") AND coalesce(fail_rate_pct,0)>=1,"warn",
    coalesce(fail_rate_pct,0)>=1 OR coalesce(dlq_docs,0)>=100000,"warn",
    isnotnull(cross_stack_gap_pct) AND cross_stack_gap_pct>=25,"warn",
    coalesce(http_ingest_status,0)>=400,"warn",
    true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="page" AND coalesce(script_compile_errors,0)>0,"remediate_script_processor_source_fix_painless_compile_errors_then_rolling_restart_ingest_path",
    severity=="page" AND coalesce(http_ingest_status,0)>=500,"inspect_coordinating_node_logs_and_load_for_ingest_pipeline_api_5xx_then_validate_disk_and_threadpools",
    severity=="page" AND coalesce(dlq_bytes,0)>1073741824,"expand_dlq_retention_policy_replay_failed_events_after_parser_fix_validate_disk_watermarks",
    severity=="warn" AND match(lower(proc_type),"grok|dissect"),"tighten_grok_or_dissect_patterns_add_on_failure_routing_to_named_dlq_index_with_processor_tag_forensics",
    severity=="warn" AND match(lower(proc_type),"geoip|dot_expander"),"validate_geoip_database_mounts_and_dot_expander_path_conflicts_on_nested_fields",
    isnotnull(cross_stack_gap_pct) AND cross_stack_gap_pct>=25,"reconcile_otel_exporter_counters_against_splunk_mirror_counts_verify_duplicate_hosts_and_sampling_divergence",
    true(),"continue_hourly_pipeline_inventory_reconciliation_and_processor_dashboard_review")
| fields - proc_type_mv fail_reason_mv baseline_eps
| table cluster_name pipeline_id tenant_id lane proc_type fail_rate_pct fail_rate_ma cluster_median_fail_pct doc_total doc_failed dlq_bytes dlq_docs otel_exporter_events splunk_mirror_events cross_stack_gap_pct fail_reason script_compile_errors http_ingest_status on_failure_policy slo_tier expected_log_family severity recommended_action last_seen
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[uc_13_2_9_es_opensearch_ingest_pipeline_reliability_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch uc_13_2_9_es_opensearch_ingest_pipeline_reliability | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize es_metrics and es_dlq arms hourly into a summary index keyed on cluster_name, pipeline_id, and tenant_id, widen operational alerts to earliest=-2h@h on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on-cluster. Compare Splunk fail_rate_pct for a pipeline.id to Kibana or OpenSearch Dashboards Ingest Pipelines UI and Dev Tools stats for the same window. When numbers disagree, verify exporter cadence, clock skew between collectors and indexers, and duplicate cluster.name labels across regions before blaming parsers.

Deep dive with GET _ingest/pipeline/<id> to read processor order, on_failure handlers, and drop processors. For each DLQ index flagged in index=es_dlq, compare dlq.bytes and dlq.docs to _cat/indices output live. Pair server log lines in index=es_logs with coordinating node identities and threadpool rejections.

Cross-stack validation: pick a tenant_id with stable traffic, compare otel_exporter_events momentum to splunk_mirror_events from tstats for the same four-hour window after normalizing baseline_eps into baseline_expected. When gap persists, inspect sampling policies on OpenTelemetry and Elasticsearch bulk responses for partial failures.

Synthetic validation: in a non-production cluster, introduce a controlled grok mismatch under change control, confirm uc_13_2_9_es_opensearch_ingest_pipeline_reliability returns warn or page with non-null recommended_action, then restore the pattern and confirm severity returns to info.

Negative test: after recovery, when node stats show zero document failures for tested pipelines, DLQ indices hold steady, server logs lack processor exceptions, and OTel mirrors Splunk within tolerance, filtering to page and warn should return zero rows across four consecutive schedules.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — grok processor failure rate climbs after an application rollout: capture processor.tag, open the pipeline definition, add temporary on_failure append handlers that route exemplar documents to a forensic DLQ index, tighten patterns with canary traffic, then replay DLQ documents after validation.

Case 2 — dissect processor failures spike on multiline events: fix line merging in Beats or Agent inputs first, then adjust dissect delimiter fields; correlate with coordinating node bulk threadpool rejections only after proving line fidelity.

Case 3 — script processor compile errors after upgrade: roll through painless source changes, validate field types referenced in scripts, and compare cluster.version across nodes to catch mixed-version ingest coordinators.

Case 4 — geoip processor misses flood logs for internal RFC1918 ranges: confirm the miss is expected, add conditional processors to skip geoip for private nets, and suppress paging for those tenants using slo_tier hints in the lookup.

Case 5 — dot_expander conflicts with existing flattened fields: inspect mapping explosions on nested objects, reorder processors so rename happens before dot_expander, and reindex only after mapping governance approves.

Case 6 — DLQ index dlq.bytes doubles overnight while application teams claim quiet releases: hunt for mis-tuned ILM delete phases, snapshot restores, or reindex tasks that replay historical failures into failed-events-* indices.

Case 7 — _ingest/pipeline REST narratives show repeated 400 responses: validate JSON bodies from automation, fix broken simulate payloads, and ensure service accounts lack destructive privileges that mask real errors.

Case 8 — coordinating node logs show 500 errors during master elections: pause parser-blame narratives, restore cluster stability, then re-evaluate ingest failures once shard availability returns.

Case 9 — OpenTelemetry exporter counters diverge from Splunk mirror counts: verify duplicate host metadata inflating one side, verify sampling differences, and verify HEC acknowledgements versus Elasticsearch bulk item errors.

Case 10 — tenant_id attribution missing after index renaming: refresh cross_stack_log_volume_baseline.csv and elasticsearch_pipeline_inventory.csv keys, then replay the search to confirm gap math uses the right tenant grain.

Case 11 — intentional on_failure routes that drop documents for cost control: document the policy in on_failure_policy, downgrade severity for those pipeline_id rows unless dlq.bytes also explodes unexpectedly.

Case 12 — false calm when es_metrics collection pauses: restore collectors before trusting negative results; compare last_seen staleness to expected five-minute cadence and page the data platform owner when freshness breaches SLO.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the machinery that prepares log records before they are stored in the search system. When parsing steps fail, dead-letter piles grow, or the same traffic no longer matches between two observability paths, we show which customer area and which step needs attention before records quietly vanish.
hardware: Applies to self-managed Elasticsearch clusters, Elastic Cloud deployments, Amazon OpenSearch Service domains, and hybrid estates where OpenTelemetry Collectors dual-feed Elasticsearch or OpenSearch plus Splunk.
